evidence: Handler classes follow *Handlers naming: SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, VisualizationHandlers — but BcqlHandlers uses a protocol acronym instead of a domain concept name, deviating from the pattern.
Utils naming split: CqlUtils and RelationUtils follow the *Utils suffix convention, but LogDiceCalculator uses *Calculator — inconsistent convention for utility/helper classes.

Model layer mixes plain class and record types: ExplorationResult is a plain class with getter methods; CoreCollocate, DiscoveredNoun, AdjectiveProfile, ExploreOptions, SingleSeedExploreOptions are records. No documented rationale for when to use each.

package-info.java exists only in the model package (model/package-info.java), not in api, config, exploration, query, utils, or indexer packages. If it exists for one, the convention should apply to all.

Exploration package has two algorithm classes: CollocateProfileComparator and MultiSeedExplorer. Both are package-private. SemanticFieldExplorer is public and delegates to both. This layering is consistent.
All production classes use PascalCase, all method names use camelCase, all package names are lowercase — Java convention is uniformly applied.

ExploreOptions vs ExplorationResult asymmetry is explicitly documented in ExploreOptions Javadoc as 'historical; the asymmetry is intentional rather than a naming error.' — acceptable documented exception.
Config package: GrammarConfig, GrammarConfigLoader, RelationConfig, RelationUtils — the pattern is Config=data, ConfigLoader=I/O, *Utils=static helpers. Consistent internal convention.

Query results: QueryResults.WordSketchResult, QueryResults.CollocateResult, QueryResults.SnippetResult are nested inside QueryResults. Exploration results (ExplorationResult, ComparisonResult) are top-level. Mixed organization for result types across layers.
All handlers are package-private classes in the api package; only WordSketchApiServer is public. Consistent visibility pattern.

strengths: Java naming conventions (PascalCase classes, camelCase methods, lowercase packages) applied uniformly with no drift.
Handler class visibility pattern is consistent: all handler classes are package-private except the server facade (WordSketchApiServer).
Config package has a coherent internal convention: *Config for data, *ConfigLoader for I/O, *Utils for static helpers.
The ExploreOptions/ExplorationResult asymmetry is documented rather than silently inconsistent.
convention_outlier.issue_character: The convention issues are isolated to three cross-cutting patterns: one handler class named after a protocol instead of a domain, one utility class using a different suffix, and one model class using a plain class instead of a record without documented rationale.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The codebase has strong within-package naming conventions and consistent Java idioms throughout. The score is held in the mid-70s by a cluster of minor deviations: BcqlHandlers naming, LogDiceCalculator suffix, plain-class vs record mix in the model layer, and the lone package-info.java. These are real but low-severity inconsistencies — any new contributor would notice them as slight irregularities without being seriously impeded.

issues.summary: BcqlHandlers uses a protocol acronym while sibling handlers use domain concept names
evidence: Sibling classes: SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, VisualizationHandlers — all named after the domain concern they serve
BcqlHandlers is named after the query language protocol (BCQL), not the domain concern (arbitrary corpus queries)
issues.suggestion: Rename to CorpusQueryHandlers or ArbitraryQueryHandlers to align with the domain-naming pattern of all sibling handler classes.

issues.summary: LogDiceCalculator uses *Calculator suffix while all other utility helpers use *Utils suffix
evidence: LogDiceCalculator uses *Calculator — single deviation in the utils package
issues.suggestion: Rename to LogDiceUtils for consistency, or adopt *Calculator as the canonical suffix and rename CqlUtils and RelationUtils to match. Either choice removes the inconsistency.

issues.summary: ExplorationResult is a plain class while most other model types are records, with no documented rationale
evidence: CoreCollocate, AdjectiveProfile, ComparisonResult, ExploreOptions, SingleSeedExploreOptions are all Java records

ExplorationResult is a plain class with private final fields and explicit getter methods — has utility methods (getTopNouns, getNounsWithMinShared, isEmpty) which justify the class, but this rationale is not documented
issues.suggestion: Add a comment in ExplorationResult explaining why it is a class rather than a record (e.g., 'plain class rather than record because it provides filtering utility methods'). Alternatively, evaluate whether it could become a record with utility as static methods on ExplorationResultUtils.

root_causes: Small deviations accumulated over iterative development without a documented decision record for which Java type (class vs record) to use for model objects.
